page.content: In the context of hematological assessments, the relationship between various analytes and the presence of splenomegaly is crucial for accurate diagnosis and treatment planning.

lines.text: In the context of hematological assessments, the relationship between various analytes and the presence of splenomegaly is
crucial for accurate diagnosis and treatment planning.

page.html:
AnalytesWarning
aPTTTest executed in the last 24h
VANCOTest executed in the last 72h
Blood countTest executed in the last 24h
CRPTest executed in the last 72h
PTTest executed in the last 24h
FERRIThis test should be repeated not before 3 months or as suggested by a specialist
TSHThis test should be repeated not before 6 months or as suggested by a specialist

page.content: The data presented in the table elucidates the distribution of specific analytes among patients with and without splenomegaly, highlighting significant variations in clinical outcomes. Notably, the column labeled “Medication (n, % of total)” provides insight into the pharmacotherapy status of the patient cohorts, revealing that a substantial proportion of the patients received no pharmacological intervention prior to testing.

page.content: This absence of medication is particularly relevant in understanding the baseline coagulation profiles, as indicated by the activated partial thromboplastin time (aPTT) values, which were executed within the last 24 hours. Furthermore, the “With splenomegaly (n=102)” and “Without splenomegaly (n=78)” columns facilitate a comparative analysis that underscores the potential impact of splenic enlargement on hemostatic function. By integrating these findings, clinicians can better interpret the implications of splenomegaly on patient management and treatment strategies, ultimately enhancing the quality of care provided to those affected by hematological disorders.

lines.text: This absence of medication is particularly relevant in understanding the baseline coagulation profiles, as indicated by the
activated partial thromboplastin time (aPTT) values, which were executed within the last 24 hours. Furthermore, the “With
splenomegaly (n=102)” and “Without splenomegaly (n=78)” columns facilitate a comparative analysis that underscores the
potential impact of splenic enlargement on hemostatic function. By integrating these findings, clinicians can better interpret the
